mnemonics: Chain Gang Gradients (CGG): Remember that Backpropagation is just the CHAIN RULE applied over and over from the output back to the input to calculate the gradient of the loss.
keyPoints: Backpropagation is an efficient algorithm for calculating the gradients of the loss function with respect to all the weights in a neural network by applying the chain rule of calculus.

Automatic Differentiation (Autodiff) is the underlying technique that decomposes a complex function into elementary operations and uses the chain rule to compute derivatives precisely, unlike symbolic or numerical differentiation.
The calculated gradients are then used by an optimization algorithm (like Gradient Descent) to update the network's weights and minimize the loss.
commonMistakes: Confusing backpropagation (the gradient calculation algorithm) with the optimization algorithm itself (e.g., Stochastic Gradient Descent), which actually uses the gradients to update the weights.

Assuming automatic differentiation is the same as numerical differentiation (which is approximate and prone to error) or symbolic differentiation (which can lead to complex and inefficient expressions).
